Title: How Google Picks Which Sentences to Cite in AI Mode
Author: Daniel Shashko
Core Claim: Google AI citations can be reverse-engineered at sentence level via text fragments.
Extracted Findings: Cited sentences skew toward the top of pages; median cited sentence is 10 words; structured pages show much higher sentence-match rates; only 25.3% of cited URLs rank in top 10 for the same query.
Operational Implications: Create early, self-contained, citable factual sentences and improve page structure.
Contradictions / Caveats: Not official guidance; methods are reverse-engineered and platform behavior can change quickly.
Tags: citations; atomic sentences; structure; Google AI Mode

Title: 38% of AI Overview Citations Pull From Top 10 Pages
Author: Ryan Law
Core Claim: Exact-query rankings now explain a smaller share of AI Overview citations than before.
Extracted Findings: Only 38% of cited URLs also rank in the top 10 for the same query, versus Ahrefs' 76% finding in July 2025.
Operational Implications: Do not optimize only for the head term; expand coverage to adjacent and fan-out subtopics.
Contradictions / Caveats: Methodology differs from other studies; overlap percentages depend on keyword set and extraction method.

Title: What is Query Fan-Out? Understanding the Hidden Mechanism Behind AI Search
Author: Louise Linehan
Core Claim: AI search systems expand one user query into multiple sub-queries before selecting sources.
Extracted Findings: Fan-out explains why source selection often goes beyond exact-query top rankings and rewards broader topical coverage.
Operational Implications: Build topic clusters and adjacent-question coverage, not single-keyword pages alone.
Contradictions / Caveats: Largely explanatory rather than a primary scientific study.

Title: Overview of OpenAI Crawlers
Author: OpenAI
Core Claim: OAI-SearchBot and GPTBot are distinct crawlers with separate controls.
Extracted Findings: Allowing or blocking one crawler does not automatically affect the other; crawl control lives in robots directives.
Operational Implications: Run crawler-access checks before blaming content quality for lack of AI visibility.

Title: Publishers and Developers FAQ
Author: OpenAI
Core Claim: Publishers allowing OAI-SearchBot can track ChatGPT referrals with utm_source=chatgpt.com.
Extracted Findings: ChatGPT referral traffic can be identified in analytics if crawler access is allowed.
Operational Implications: Add analytics instrumentation and track AI-sourced visits separately from classic organic search.
Contradictions / Caveats: OpenAI-specific; traffic patterns depend on vertical and query type.

Title: Top ways to ensure your content performs well in Google's AI experiences
Author: Google
Core Claim: Google recommends unique, non-commodity content that genuinely satisfies user needs.
Extracted Findings: Users in AI search ask longer, more specific and follow-up questions; helpfulness and uniqueness remain central.
Operational Implications: Reject generic paraphrase pages; prioritize original insight and decision-useful content.
Contradictions / Caveats: High-level guidance rather than a tactical checklist.

Title: SAGEO Arena: A Realistic Environment for Evaluating Search-Augmented Generative Engine Optimization
Author: Kim et al.
Sample Size / Scope: Large-scale benchmark with structural web documents
Core Claim: Realistic GEO must consider retrieval, reranking and generation together, not generation alone.
Extracted Findings: Existing approaches often degrade performance under realistic conditions; structural information such as schema helps; optimization must be stage-specific.
Operational Implications: Separate discoverability rules from citation-selection rules and answer-inclusion rules.
Contradictions / Caveats: Preprint; benchmark environment may not map perfectly to every production system.

Title: Generative Engine Optimization: A VLM and Agent Framework for Pinterest Acquisition Growth
Author: Zhang et al.
Sample Size / Scope: Production deployment across billions of images and tens of millions of collections
Core Claim: Pinterest built GEO around query prediction, collection pages and authority-aware interlinking.
Extracted Findings: Reported 20% organic traffic growth tied to semantically coherent collection pages and improved interlinking.
Operational Implications: Use clusters, aggregations and interlinking as first-class GEO levers, not afterthoughts.
Contradictions / Caveats: Single-company case study; platform characteristics differ from B2B publishing.

Title: AI Overviews Reduce Clicks by 34.5%
Author: Patrick Stox
Core Claim: AI Overviews can materially reduce traditional click-through rates.
Extracted Findings: Position-one pages saw lower click-through rates when AI Overviews were present.
Operational Implications: Measure visibility, citations and assisted outcomes, not only classic CTR.
Contradictions / Caveats: Older than 2026 and may evolve as AI Overviews change.

Title: Semrush Report: AI Overviews' Impact on Search in 2025
Author: Semrush
Sample Size / Scope: 10M+ keywords; 200K+ keywords for zero-click trends; 11K domains
Core Claim: AI Overviews expanded and shifted beyond purely informational queries during 2025.
Extracted Findings: Semrush found changes in intent mix, SERP volatility and zero-click behavior as AI Overviews evolved.
Operational Implications: Use this as a monitoring source for trigger patterns and vertical volatility.
Contradictions / Caveats: Useful for trend monitoring; not as foundational as official docs or primary research papers.

Title: AI in Search: Going beyond information to intelligence
Author: Google
Core Claim: Google says AI Overviews drove over 10% query growth in some markets for qualifying query classes.
Extracted Findings: AI search experiences can expand usage and encourage more complex searching behavior.
Operational Implications: Assume search behavior is getting more conversational and iterative; build content for multi-step exploration.
Contradictions / Caveats: Product blog; high-level product framing rather than optimization detail.

Title: 76% of AI Overview Citations Pull From Top 10 Pages
Author: Ryan Law
Core Claim: Earlier 2025 data showed a much tighter overlap between rankings and AI citations.
Extracted Findings: 76.1% of cited pages ranked in the top 10 in the earlier study, creating a useful before-versus-after comparison with March 2026.
Operational Implications: Store historical baselines so your doctrine can detect shifts instead of freezing one moment as permanent truth.
